__fixture__.name: source_control_critical_action_parity
__fixture__.scenario: A Source Control push/publish action is visible from a sidebar selection. The fixture requires the action to resolve through a canonical command, stay reachable from a keyboard-first route, and escalate to review instead of existing only as a private sidebar row affordance.

candidate_summary: Provider-visible push/publish action offered from a selected branch or outgoing-change row.
decision_factors: The action crosses a provider-visible boundary and cannot be committed from a private sidebar-only button.
The Source Control sidebar may offer the selected-row affordance, but the command descriptor owns enablement, preview, approval, and result evidence.
A keyboard-first command-palette route is required in addition to the sidebar/context-menu route.
Review or sheet presentation must preserve target branch, remote/provider boundary, policy state, and failure recovery.

required_command_bindings.notes: Push/publish remains command-backed and escalates to review or sheet when preview, policy, provider, or authority truth requires it.

duplicate_surface_analysis: Source Control owns the structural branch/change row. The action may be projected into sidebar selection and context menu slots, but the command graph and review surface own the mutation path, preventing a private sidebar-only publish affordance.
